note: All rules verified against the live wiki. 'verified: true' means name and CWE confirmed. Rules without CWE in the wiki have an empty cwe array with a note.

rules.name: Do not suppress or ignore checked exceptions
rules.text: Catch blocks MUST either recover from the exception, rethrow it, or throw a context-appropriate exception. Empty catch blocks are forbidden.

rules.name: Do not complete abruptly from a finally block
rules.text: Finally blocks MUST NOT exit abruptly via return, break, continue, or by throwing exceptions, as this discards pending exceptions.

rules.name: Do not let checked exceptions escape from a finally block
rules.text: Finally blocks MUST handle or rethrow checked exceptions; escaping checked exceptions suppress the original exception.

rules.name: Do not throw RuntimeException, Exception, or Throwable
rules.text: Code MUST throw specific exception types, not generic RuntimeException, Exception, or Throwable.

rules.name: Do not catch NullPointerException or any of its ancestors
rules.text: Code MUST NOT rely on catching NullPointerException — null checks MUST be performed explicitly.
rules.note: No formal CWE mapping table on the live wiki page.

rules.name: Adopt and implement a consistent and comprehensive error-handling policy
rules.text: All functions that return an error indicator MUST have their return values checked and handled.

rules.name: Prefer functions that support error checking over equivalent functions that don't
rules.text: Functions that provide error detection MUST be preferred over those that silently fail.
rules.note: Live wiki lists 8 CWEs (20, 79, 89, 91, 94, 114, 601, 676). CWE-676 is the primary semantic match.

rules.name: Avoid deadlock by requesting and releasing locks in the same order
rules.text: Multiple locks MUST always be acquired in a consistent predefined order to prevent circular wait conditions.

rules.name: Ensure actively held locks are released on exceptional conditions
rules.text: Locks MUST be released in finally blocks to guarantee release even when exceptions occur.

rules.name: Notify all waiting threads rather than a single thread
rules.text: notifyAll() MUST be used instead of notify() when multiple threads may be waiting on the same condition.

rules.name: Always invoke wait() and await() methods inside a loop
rules.text: wait() and await() MUST be called inside a loop that re-checks the condition to handle spurious wakeups.
rules.note: No CWE mapping listed on the live wiki page.

rules.name: Avoid deadlock by locking in a predefined order
rules.text: Mutexes MUST always be acquired in a consistent predefined order across all threads.

rules.name: Wrap functions that can spuriously wake up in a loop
rules.text: Condition variable waits MUST be wrapped in a loop that re-checks the predicate after wakeup.
rules.note: No CWE mapping listed on the live wiki page.

rules.name: Close files when they are no longer needed
rules.text: File handles MUST be closed promptly after use; resource leaks degrade availability.

rules.name: Free dynamically allocated memory when no longer needed
rules.text: Dynamically allocated memory MUST be freed when no longer needed to prevent resource exhaustion.
